intent: Open the broken images page. All images on the page should load successfully.
step: Check that every image on the page loads and is not broken
observed_behavior:
The page contains three images, but two of them are intentionally broken:
1. "asdf.jpg" - does not exist (broken image)
2. "hjkl.jpg" - does not exist (broken image)
3. "img/avatar-blank.jpg" - may be valid

The page is titled "Broken Images" and is specifically designed to display broken images. The first two image sources reference non-existent files (asdf.jpg and hjkl.jpg), which means they will fail to load. This contradicts the requirement that "all images on the page should load successfully." The page itself cannot meet this requirement as it's intentionally designed to show broken images.
agent_reasoning: The user wants me to check that every image on the broken_images page loads successfully. Looking at the DOM, I can see there are 3 images in the main content: 1. - This appears to be a broken link (non-existent file) 2. - This also appears to be a broken link (non-existent file) 3. - This might be valid The page is already open at https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/broken_images. The intent is to verify that ALL images load successfully, but based on the image sources, it's clear that the first two images (asdf.jpg and hjkl.jpg) are intentionally broken - they don't point to valid image files. This is a test page specifically designed to demonstrate broken images. The task asks me to verify that all images load, but the page itself is designed with broken images. This appears to be a contradiction - the page's purpose is to show broken images, not to have all images working. I should report this as a failure since the page does not meet the requirement that "all images on the page should load successfully."
